THE STREAM GAUGE ON TURKEY BRANCH NEAR ROCKVILLE ROSE FIVE FEET IN FIFTEEN MINUTES...QUICKLY EXCEEDING FLOOD STAGE AND FLOODING AREA ROADWAYS. THE STREAM THEN FELL BELOW FLOOD STAGE BUT FLOODED TWICE MORE DURING THE AFTERNOON AS MORE HEAVY RAIN AFFECTED THE AREA.
THE STREAM GAUGE ON GWYNNS FALLS AT VILLA NOVA EXCEEDED FLOOD STAGE OF 11 FEET...FLOODING NEARBY ROADWAYS. THE STREAM ROSE TWO FEET IN AN HOUR AROUND THE TIME IT EXCEEDED FLOOD STAGE.

THE STREAM GAUGE ON SAWMILL CREEK EXCEEDED ITS FLOOD STAGE OF 5 FEET. NUMEROUS ROADS WERE CLOSED AND FLOODING WAS REPORTED IN TWO BUSINESSES. THIS IS THE HIGHEST CREEK LEVEL EVER RECORDED AT THIS LOCATION. CONTINUOUS RECORDS DATE BACK TO 1983.
OUTER LOOP OF THE BALTIMORE BELTWAY FLOODED AND COMPLETELY CLOSED AT QUARANTINE ROAD. ALL TRAFFIC DIVERTED.

BALTIMORE HARBOR TUNNEL CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
WATER RESCUE PERFORMED ON SURRATTS ROAD AT THE FOX RUN CROSSING NEAR BRANCH AVENUE.
MORE THAN SIX INCHES OF WATER FLOWING OVER TIMBERBROOK DRIVE IN WALDORF. YARDS ALSO FLOODED WITH WATER NEAR A HOME.
AT LEAST SIX INCHES OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS BENSON AVENUE IN ARBUTUS.
WATER RESCUES FROM HOMES. THREE FEET OF WATER REPORTED IN A BUSINESS NEAR PISCATAWAY CREEK ON BRANDYWINE ROAD.

BALTIMORE ANNAPOLIS BOULEVARD WAS FLOODED AND CLOSED AT THE MAGOTHY RIVER CROSSING NEAR PASADENA.
WATER RESCUE ON WEST STREET AT HUDSON STREET. CAR STRANDED IN THREE FEET OF WATER.
SLIGO CREEK OVERFLOWED ITS BANKS. A PORTION OF SLIGO CREEK PARKWAY WAS CLOSED. A WATER RESCUE HAD TO BE PERFORMED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SLIGO CREEK PARKWAY AND KENNEBEC AVENUE.
